title: use-after-free of live Uri\WhatWg\Url objects via periodic lexbor mraw clean
category: use-after-free across the PHP ↔ lexbor (C) boundary (shared arena cleaned out from under live objects)
crash_site: segfault / heap-use-after-free reading the data of a live Uri\WhatWg\Url object after a number of URL parses in the same request. The object access is valid PHP; its backing memory was reclaimed.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: php_uri_parser_whatwg_free (the per-object teardown the fix ADDS — correct) and the RSHUTDOWN handler. The defect is the periodic lexbor_mraw_clean() that reclaims memory still owned by live Url objects.
ground_truth.fix_summary: The WhatWG URI parser periodically called lexbor_mraw_clean(lexbor_parser.mraw), destroying the data backing any live Uri\WhatWg\Url object -> UAF. Fix: remove the periodic lexbor_mraw_clean(); add php_uri_parser_whatwg_free() to free per object; move lexbor teardown from RSHUTDOWN to POST_ZEND_DEACTIVATE so frees run before the arena is destroyed.

admission.note: Authored from PR #19591 body+diff. crash != cause: UAF reading a live Url object; cause is a periodic lexbor_mraw_clean() in the WhatWG parser lifecycle reclaiming the shared lexbor arena that live PHP Url objects still point into. Decoy = php_uri_parser_whatwg_free (the correct teardown the fix introduces). PHP object lifetime <-> lexbor (C) arena. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/php/php-src/pull/19591 — uri: Fix lexbor memory management in uri_parser_whatwg.c; fix 423960aad30b, base 90822f7692e7.
